concerns.text: Phase 7 (Tasks T045-T054) introduces Cellular Automata (Rule 30, 110) and comparative analysis of computational classes. This directly contradicts FR-001 and User Story 1 in spec.md, which strictly limit scope to 'canonical chaotic systems like the Lorenz attractor' (Lorenz/Rössler). The spec contains no provision for discrete rule-space systems; this is unapproved scope creep violating the spec boundary.

Task T031 ('Identify Critical Threshold') is marked with an unchecked box [ ] and lacks a defined completion artifact, yet it is the sole task responsible for implementing FR-008 ('identify critical SNR threshold') and SC-003 ('FNN saturation point measured against SNR level where FNN rate exceeds 50%'). Without a valid, executable task to produce this logic, these requirements have zero implementation coverage.

Tasks T037 and T038 are marked with unchecked boxes [ ] and missing artifacts. T037 is required to verify the pipeline runtime against the 2-hour constraint (FR-010), and T038 is required to write the final results. The spec mandates measuring runtime against the 2-hour budget (SC-005), but the task list currently has no implemented logic to verify or enforce this constraint.

Task T048 requires implementing 'CA-to-continuous mapping logic' for Cellular Automata. However, spec.md Key Entities only define 'ChaoticTrajectory' and 'NoisyTrajectory' for continuous systems. There is no defined schema or entity for discrete Cellular Automata trajectories in the spec, making the data model for Phase 7 unaligned with the specification and violating the Single Source of Truth principle.
